body.title: Granny: generic availability semaphore + fix THROTTLED false positive
body.result: availability.py semaphore protocol; killed _launch_cc_instance spawn path; _cc0_in_progress() replaces tmux count; GRANNY_THROTTLED false-positive eliminated
body.description:
Granny currently uses _count_active_cc_sessions() (counts tmux cc-* sessions) to throttle dispatch — this produces false positives (GRANNY_THROTTLED|active=1 even with no CC workers). Replace with two changes: (1) a generic is_available(worker_id) function reading ~/.granny/available/{worker_id}.available.true/false — .false present wins regardless of .true; (2) replace 'busy' check with Postgres query status=in_progress AND worker={id} instead of tmux count.

Semaphore directory ~/.granny/available/ created on first use. Universal protocol: any device/worker drops {name}.available.true to register, {name}.available.false to block (e.g., granny.queue.available.false pauses Granny's own dispatch loop).

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (remove _count_active_cc_sessions, _list_cc_sessions; update top of run_once), new devices/granny/availability.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every availability state change at INFO
**Scope boundary:** IN: is_available(worker_id) pure function; .false-wins logic; in_progress Postgres query replaces tmux count; GRANNY_THROTTLED false positive fixed. OUT: time-window rules (T-granny-dispatch-config), CC.0 actual dispatch (T-granny-cc0-dispatch).
**Completion criteria:** GRANNY_THROTTLED no longer fires when no real CC session is active; is_available('CC.0') returns True with only .true present, False with .false present, False with neither; unit tests covering all three states plus the granny.queue namespace.
